# nodum

> Atomic-notes knowledge system: a mutable PostgreSQL graph of typed, UUID-keyed nodes and edges over a runtime-evolvable schema, with full-text search and recursive subgraph expansion. Every node has a plain-text `content` body plus a JSON `data` payload. Kinds (node + edge) are stored in the database and editable at runtime. One data-service layer is fronted by a CLI, an HTTP API, and a React single-page web UI. Built to be driven by LLM agents: the CLI and the API emit byte-identical JSON, and `nodum schema` (or `GET /schema`) dumps the whole machine-readable schema contract. The full app ships as a Docker image; the PyPI wheel is the CLI/library (no UI). Retrieval is full-text + graph only — no embeddings yet (`content` is stored ready for them).

## Docs

- [Overview](https://nodum.vcoeur.com/): what nodum is, what it does, and who it is for.
- [Quick start](https://nodum.vcoeur.com/quick-start/): run the full app with Docker (or install the CLI), sign in, then create a typed node, link a typed edge, search, and expand a subgraph.
- [Concepts](https://nodum.vcoeur.com/concepts/): the typed graph — nodes, edges, the runtime-evolvable schema of DB-stored kinds, the from→to edge signatures, soft service validation vs cheap-hard DB constraints, and the open-process-closed-format principle.
- [Install & run](https://nodum.vcoeur.com/install/): the Docker (full app) and PyPI (CLI/library) tracks, all environment variables, authentication, and migrating an older database.
- [Commands](https://nodum.vcoeur.com/commands/): full CLI verb + HTTP API route reference, the `--set` field syntax, the byte-identical JSON envelopes, and the error contract.

## For agents

- Run `nodum schema` (CLI) or `GET /schema` (API) for the contract: every node kind with its field schema, every edge kind with its `from → to` signature. One call self-orients an agent without reading these docs — and it reflects the live, possibly-edited catalog.
- The CLI and the API call the same service and emit byte-identical JSON; prototype with the CLI, ship against the API. The CLI prints one JSON object to stdout; messages and errors go to stderr.
- Kinds are stored in the database and editable at runtime (7 seeded node kinds, 12 edge kinds, not a fixed set): `nodum node-kind add/edit/rm`, `nodum edge-kind add/edit/rm`, or `POST/PATCH/DELETE /node-kinds` and `/edge-kinds`. Deleting an in-use kind is refused unless you reassign its rows with `--into`/`?into=` (or, for an edge kind, remove its edges with `--purge`/`?purge=true`); `schema` reports each kind's `usage` count. Every node carries a plain-text `content` body (full-text indexed) plus its typed `data` fields (`str`, `int`, `float`, `bool`, `list[str]`, `enum`, `date`, `datetime`; a `datetime` is stored canonically as UTC ISO-8601 with a `Z` suffix and shown/entered in local time by the web UI). Edge endpoints are checked against the kind's signature at create time.
- Data routes require auth (single main password): send the token from `POST /auth/login` as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. A 503 means no password is set yet; a 401 means unauthenticated. The local CLI is trusted and talks to the database directly.

## Optional

- [Source on GitHub](https://github.com/vcoeur/nodum)
- [nodum on PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/nodum/)
